VAL-MIGRATE-001.evidence: All tests in 'VAL-MIGRATE-001: Legacy reminded status mapped' describe block passed. Test 'migration 004 maps reminded -> in_progress' confirms SQL contains UPDATE todos SET status='in_progress' WHERE status='reminded'. Test 'migration 004 preserves timestamp via reminded_at = updated_at' confirms reminded_at is preserved. Supporting tests in '004_backfill_status.sql' describe block (9/9 passed) verify the mapping is correct, idempotent, and non-destructive.

VAL-MIGRATE-002.evidence: All tests in 'VAL-MIGRATE-002: Migration is idempotent' describe block passed. Test '004 can be applied twice without error' confirms both UPDATEs have WHERE clauses targeting old status values so second run is a no-op. Test '005 can be applied twice without error' confirms DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS pattern and status != 'cleanup_deleted' exclusion on all UPDATEs. Additional idempotency tests in both migration describe blocks (004: 'should produce the same result when applied twice', 005: 'should produce the same result when applied twice') also passed.

VAL-MIGRATE-003.evidence: All tests in 'VAL-MIGRATE-003: Polluted rows cleaned' describe block passed. Test '005 identifies [PT] agent messages in task text' confirms LIKE '%[PT]%' pattern. Test '005 identifies duplicate todos created within 5 seconds' confirms INTERVAL '5 seconds' duplicate detection. Test '005 uses reversible soft-delete (cleanup_deleted) not permanent deletion' confirms UPDATE SET status='cleanup_deleted' (not DELETE). Supporting tests verify constraint widening, original preservation (t.created_at > dup.created_at), and user_id/task matching.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All 39 tests in data-migration-cleanup.test.ts passed (204ms). Migration SQL files (004_backfill_status.sql, 005_cleanup_polluted.sql) are well-structured with idempotent WHERE clauses, reversible soft-delete approach, and correct status mappings.
